questions: The package's `src/runpod_lifecycle/__init__.py` was not readable from this session (sandboxing). The brief names the exports it expects (`RunPodConfig.from_env`, `launch`, `terminate`, `api.get_pod_status`, `api.create_pod`, `api.get_pod_ssh_details`, `get_network_volumes`, `list_pods`, `find_orphans`). Is there also an SSH helper (class `SSHClient` or equivalent) exported? `scripts/uv_e2e_test.py` and `scripts/uv_lock_benchmark.py` currently import `SSHClient` from `gpu_orchestrator.runpod.ssh` — if the package doesn't export an SSH helper, they'll need a v0.1.1 re-export or a consumer shim.

Inventory of reigh-worker-orchestrator found only `requirements-dev.txt` (pytest/coverage). No `pyproject.toml` was surfaced. The brief says `pip install -e .[dev]` — is there a `pyproject.toml` or `setup.py` I should add the git dep to, or should I add `runpod-lifecycle @ git+...` to a top-level `requirements.txt` (which also needs confirmation of location)?

`control_loop.py` uses private storage-mixin methods that aren't in the brief's call-site list: `self.runpod._get_storage_volume_id(storage_name)` at :1480, `self.runpod.check_storage_health(...)` at :1502, `self.runpod._expand_network_volume(volume_id, new_size)` at :1517. The brief says preserve storage-volume fallback as it's live-tested. Does the new package expose equivalents (storage health + expand), or should the adapter keep these as local methods using the RunPod SDK directly?

reigh-worker has uncommitted changes per the brief. Is it acceptable for me to `git stash` them, branch from clean main, then pop — or should I confirm the dirty file list with you first in case there's overlap with migration targets (e.g., `debug/commands/storage.py`)?

The orchestrator has its own `scripts/debug/commands/runpod.py` (direct `import runpod` SDK usage at line 14). The brief only mentions the reigh-worker copy. Should the orchestrator copy also be migrated to `runpod_lifecycle.list_pods` / `find_orphans` in this pass, or leave it alone?

The adapter's home — should it live at `gpu_orchestrator/worker_spawner.py` (brief's suggestion) or is there a more natural fit (e.g., keep it inside `gpu_orchestrator/runpod/__init__.py` alongside the surviving `startup_script.py`)? The brief prefers the former; flagging in case you have an architectural preference.

success_criteria.criterion: GitHub repo `banodoco/runpod-lifecycle` exists, is public, shows the description 'Reusable async RunPod lifecycle, discovery, and CLI for the Banodoco stack.', and has the `v0.1.0` tag visible.
`cd /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-worker-orchestrator && pytest -q tests/` exits 0 (green) on the `runpod-lifecycle-migration` branch.
In reigh-worker-orchestrator, `grep -r 'from gpu_orchestrator.runpod' --include='*.py'` returns only imports from `gpu_orchestrator.runpod.startup_script` (no other submodules remain referenced).
`gpu_orchestrator/runpod/api.py`, `client.py`, `lifecycle.py`, `storage.py`, `ssh.py` are deleted from the orchestrator; `startup_script.py` still exists.
`tests/gpu_orchestrator/runpod/test_startup_script.py` is KEPT (still present, still green) — it tests the module that stays in-tree.
A new file `gpu_orchestrator/worker_spawner.py` exists and exposes the adapter class used by `control_loop.py`.
No commits land on `main` of either `reigh-worker-orchestrator` or `reigh-worker`. Changes live on branches named `runpod-lifecycle-migration` pushed to origin.
No secrets (contents of `.env`, API keys, RunPod tokens) appear in any commit across the three repos. `git log -p` / search for `RUNPOD_API_KEY=` literal values returns nothing.
No `.venv/`, `.pytest_cache/`, or `__pycache__/` directories are committed to the `runpod-lifecycle` repo.
The reigh-worker uncommitted changes present before migration remain unmodified after migration (same files dirty, same diff content — verified via `git status` comparison).

Consumer imports map exactly per the brief: `create_runpod_client()` → `RunPodConfig.from_env()`, `terminate_worker` → `runpod_lifecycle.terminate`, `get_pod_status` → `runpod_lifecycle.api.get_pod_status`, `create_pod_and_wait` → `runpod_lifecycle.api.create_pod`, `get_pod_ssh_details` → `runpod_lifecycle.api.get_pod_ssh_details`, `get_network_volumes` → `runpod_lifecycle.get_network_volumes`.

Adapter preserves: RAM-tier fallback in spawn, storage-volume fallback in spawn, and startup-script injection from `gpu_orchestrator/runpod/startup_script.py`. Code paths remain structurally identical to pre-migration.
`control_loop.py`'s 23 observed `self.runpod.*` call sites all resolve against the new adapter surface — no AttributeError on import or instantiation.
If package exports had gaps, a `v0.1.1` tag exists on GitHub and both consumer repos pin to `@v0.1.1`; otherwise both pin to `@v0.1.0`.
Adapter file (`worker_spawner.py`) is under ~400 lines and contains no unrelated refactors — purely a translation layer over `runpod_lifecycle` + Supabase writes + fallbacks.
Live smoke: spawn a worker, observe it reaches Ready, terminate it. Exercises RAM-tier + storage-volume + startup-script paths that unit tests don't cover.

assumptions: Assumption: package `src/runpod_lifecycle/__init__.py` exports at least `RunPodConfig`, `launch`, `terminate`, `list_pods`, `find_orphans`, `get_network_volumes`, plus submodules `api` (with `create_pod`, `get_pod_status`, `get_pod_ssh_details`) and `ssh` (with an `SSHClient`-equivalent) — matching the brief's prescribed call shapes. Gaps fall back to Step 9 (v0.1.1 bump) or a consumer shim.
Assumption: runpod-lifecycle's existing test suite is already green (the brief says `expect 50+ passed`), so the package publish step treats it as a gate, not a fix-up phase.

Assumption: `startup_script.py` stays in `gpu_orchestrator/runpod/` with a trimmed `__init__.py` that only re-exports from it; I will not move it to a new location just to eliminate the subdirectory (minimizing diff in preserved paths).

Assumption: `tests/gpu_orchestrator/runpod/test_startup_script.py` (89 lines, tests the surviving module) is KEPT — not included in the bulk delete even though it matches the brief's `test_*.py` glob under that directory.

Assumption: the orchestrator's `WorkerSpawnerAdapter` preserves the exact method names used today (`spawn_worker`, `start_worker_process`, `terminate_worker`, `check_and_initialize_worker`, `get_pod_status`, `generate_worker_id`, `check_storage_health`, `_get_storage_volume_id`, `_expand_network_volume`) so `control_loop.py`'s ~23 call sites need no behavioral rewrite — only the constructor at line 44 changes.

Assumption: migration branches on both consumer repos are named `runpod-lifecycle-migration` (per brief) and only pushed to `origin`; no PR opened automatically. The human opens PRs after reviewing the branch.

Assumption: `reigh-worker`'s uncommitted changes do not overlap with the migration targets (`scripts/live_test/config.py`, `scripts/live_test/variant_fresh.py`, `scripts/live_test/variant_update.py`, `debug/commands/storage.py`, `debug/commands/runpod.py`). If overlap exists, the plan pauses until the human resolves it.
Assumption: `gh` CLI is authenticated with write access to the `banodoco` org — `gh repo create banodoco/...` will succeed. If not, Phase 1 pauses.

Assumption: `v0.1.0` installs cleanly via `pip install 'runpod-lifecycle @ git+https://github.com/banodoco/runpod-lifecycle@v0.1.0'` on both consumer repos' Python versions (package supports 3.11+; orchestrator is likely 3.11 or 3.12).
